publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-06 00:01:39 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, lecture and research notes, course materials, financial and subject files, manuscripts, printed materials, photographs, diagrams, glass slides and negatives, movie film, tape recording.
did.abstract: Personal and professional papers and materials of Cornell University professor of psychobiology, founder of the Behavior Farm Laboratory.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

eventgrp.event: Cornell Univ. Chairman of Dept. of Physiology in the Medical College

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Personal papers include school notebooks, 1920's, and miscellany. Professional papers include correspondence, acknowledgements, recommendations for students, Department of Psychology and Behavior Farm Laboratory correspondence; conference and lecture notes, manuscripts, programs, and memos, 1952-1963; student notes, rollbooks, and course materials; research notebooks and files, ca. 1930-1959; Department of Psychology financial files, 1926-1963, and subject files on topics including the Behavior Farm, equipment, and procedures; manuscripts and printed materials include reprints by Liddell and other authors; also visual materials such as photograph albums, diagrams, glass slides and negatives, and movie film dealing with pig, sheep, dog, and goat experiments at the Behavior Farm. Correspondents include the American Russian Institute, American Society for Research in Psychosomatic Problems, Association for Advancement of Psychoanalysis, W. Horsley Gantt, A. U. Moore, Leon J. Saul, J. Paul Scott, graduate students, publishers, and other scientists and psychologists.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Howard S. Liddell Papers, #14-23-1466. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Psychology -- Study and teaching.
Psychology -- Research.
controlaccess.corpname: American Society for Research in Psychosomatic Problems
Association for the Advancement of Psychoanalysis

did.unittitle: Notebook: Prof. Simpson's lectures on the Endocrine Organs, March 1920
Exam Book: Notes on Endocrine Organs
Notebook: Physiology of Nervous System, Vol. 2
Liddell Memorial Address by F. S. Freeman, 1962
History and Prospects of the Behavior Farm Laboratory at Cornell, by Howard Liddell
American Society for Research in Psychosomatic Problems
American Society for Research in Psychosomatic Problems - Cttees.
Annual Review of Physiology (T. M. Luck - editor)
Kardiner (Assoc. for Research in Psychoanalysis & Exp. Psychodynamics)
Selinsky - Assoc. for Adv. of Psychoanalysis
Neurotic Behavior in Animals and Man
Problems in Social Medicine, Gertrude Hanchett, 1933
Psychology 133 Term Report, Alan M. Levy, 1947
Physiological Stress and the Gen. Adoption Syndrome, H. D. Arbitman, ca. 1949
Response to Stress of Modified early Development, M. Amstey, 1962
Report of Progress in C. R. Studies, 1931
"The Hypnosis of Animals" by H. Dexter, Translated by H. Schulman and M. Stern, 1934
External Inhibition or Negative Induction in the Cerebral Cortex, 1935
Observations on Experimental Neurosis in Sheep, 1935
Effect of Cortin on Experimental Neurosis in Sheep, 1935
New Approach to Problem of Experimental Neurosis, 1936
Nervous Strain in Domesticated Animals and in Man, 1936
A Breathing Mark for Animals of Medium Size, 1937
EEG's and their Interpretation/Interpretation of Electrical Activity in the Brain, 1937
Effect of Cortin in Glycerine Orally Administered on the Experimental Neurosis in Sheep, 1937
Experimental Neurosis in the Pig - Quin F. Curtis, 1937
Preliminary Comment on Investigation of Patterns of Activity, [1937]
Project for Committee on Problems of Neurotic Behavior, 1938
Goldman Thesis: "Experimental Investigation of Behavior of Goat by Conditioned Reflex Method," 1939
Direct Current Potentials Associated with Human Ovulation, 1939
Parmenter - D. C. Potentials Associated with Ovulation and Fatigue, 1940
Role of Animal Experimentation in Psychiatry As Illustrated by Experimental Neurosis, 1940
W. T. James - Transfer of Conditioned Avoiding Reaction/Significance of Work on CMR, 1940
[The Conditioned Reflex Experiment - Pony as Subject] with photos, ca. 1940
W. T. James - Report on Nutrition in Dogs, 1941
Marcuse and Moore - Heart Rate and Respiration Preceding and Following Audiogenic Seizures in White Rat, 1941
Hunt Handbook Chapter: Conditioned Reflex Method and Experimental Neurosis, 1942
Animal Behavior Studies Bearing on the Problem of Pain, 1943
References - Experimental Production of Behavior Derangement
Important Developments in Am. Soc. Psych. in Past Decade
Lindemann and Finesinger: "Response of Psychoneurotics to Adrenalin and Mecholyl"
Morphological Form and its Relation to Reflex Action and Behavior
Conditioned Salivary Reflex and its Inhibition
"Effect of Post-Partum Separation of Mother and Kid on Maternal Care in the Domestic Goat"
Autopsies on Experimental Animals - Behavior Farm Lab
"Emotional Hazards in Animals and Man" by H. Liddell
Reprints - report by World Health Organization
Reprints - Salpeter, Lyman, Block, Koella, Samuels, Tobach, Bowlby, Goldfarb
Prof. Liddell with sheep and unidentified man, ca. 1962
Photos used in Effect of LSD-25 - on the walking activity & pattern in the goat
Experiments on palatability of good constituents - with photos & graphs - by W. T. James
Figures for Respiratory "Fingerprint" of Nervous States - Sutherland, Wolf, and Kennedy, 1937
Pig - also contains set of slides of cat experiments labeled from University of Chicago
Slides of apparatus and goats and sheep experiments
Slides of apparatus and experimental results, ca. 1920-30

did.physdesc: Note: torn emulsion on negative in slot #28

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Films on animal behavior (cats, pigs, dogs, rats, goats) are produced at the Behavior Farm Lab. H.S, Liddell, and A.U. Moore.

did.unittitle: National Pocket Roll Book and Cornell University official Examination on Book, Notes
Series VIII. Tape recording and Phonograph album
